pattern: Membership testing is the only operation this problem needs, so converting both arrays into sets turns 'find what's missing' into a direct set subtraction.
hints: You don't need to compare every pair of elements -- you only need to know, for each distinct value, whether it appears anywhere in the other array at all.
Build a set from each array; anything in the first set that's absent from the second set belongs in the first output list, and symmetrically for the second.
Set difference gives you exactly the distinct values unique to each array in one pass, with no need to track counts or positions.

edge_inputs.description: both arrays minimum length with no overlap
identical arrays share everything, both differences empty
duplicates collapse to a single distinct entry
disjoint arrays with negative and positive values
one array is fully contained in the other
extreme negative and positive values at the constraint edge

stress.description: two arrays at the constraint maximum length with overlapping value ranges
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    m = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    nums1 = [rng.randint(-1000, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    nums2 = [rng.randint(-1000, 1000) for _ in range(m)]
    return [nums1, nums2]
